meta.title: So y such
meta.goal: Ya puedes usar so y such para intensificar adjetivos, adverbios y sustantivos.

options: It was so a cold day.
It was such a cold day.
quiz.explain: such + a/an + adjetivo + sustantivo singular: such a cold day. so va solo con adjetivos/adverbios sin sustantivo: It was so cold.

quiz.q: Elige la opción correcta: 'They have ___ good friends.'
quiz.explain: friends es un sustantivo plural, así que usamos such (sin artículo): such good friends. so no va directamente antes de un sustantivo.
so + adverbio + that = resultado: She spoke so quickly that nobody understood. such + (a/an) + adjetivo + sustantivo + that.

cards.title: so + adjetivo o adverbio
cards.lede: so intensifica adjetivos y adverbios solos, sin sustantivo después. Es equivalente a «tan» en español.

rows.value: The food was so good! · I'm so tired.
This book is so interesting. · He's so tall.
She speaks so quickly. · He drives so slowly.
You did so well! · It happened so suddenly.

tip.label: Regla clave:
tip.body: Si después de «tan» en español no hay sustantivo («tan cansado», «tan rápido»), usa so.

cards.title: such + a/an + adjetivo + sustantivo
cards.lede: such intensifica una frase nominal completa con sustantivo singular contable. Siempre lleva a o an.

rows.value: It was such a nice day!
She is such a good teacher.
He made such an interesting point.
That was such an awful experience.

tip.label: Estructura:
tip.body: such + a/an + ADJETIVO + SUSTANTIVO. Nunca olvides el artículo con singular: such a, such an (nunca solo «such»).

cards.title: such con plurales e incontables
cards.lede: Con sustantivos plurales o incontables, such no lleva artículo. Este es un error muy común.

rows.value: She gave me such good advice.

tip.label: Resumen:
tip.body: singular contable → such a/an. plural e incontable → such (sin artículo).

cards.title: ¿so o such? Elige el correcto
cards.lede: La clave está en lo que sigue: ¿solo un adjetivo/adverbio? → so. ¿Adjetivo + sustantivo? → such.

columns.label: such
examples: It's such a hot day. (day = sustantivo)
She's such a fast runner. (runner = sustantivo)
It was such a boring film.

tip.label: Prueba rápida:
tip.body: ¿Puedo añadir un sustantivo después? Si sí → such. Si no → so. «so hot» (no hay sustantivo) vs. «such a hot day» (day es el sustantivo).

cards.title: so much, so many, so few, so little
cards.lede: so también se combina con cuantificadores. La elección depende de si el sustantivo es contable o incontable.

rows.value: There are so many people here!
She has so many shoes. · I have so many things to do.
I ate so much food. · There's so much noise.
I don't have so much time. · She has so much patience.
There are so few seats left!
We have so little time. · There's so little water.

cards.title: so…that / such…that
cards.lede: Para expresar un resultado o consecuencia, conecta la intensidad con that. Es la estructura de «tan... que».

lines.text: It was so cold that we stayed inside.
She was so tired that she fell asleep.
He spoke so fast that I couldn't understand.
It was such a good movie that I watched it twice.
lines.note: such + a + adj + sustantivo + that + resultado

lines.text: They had such bad luck that they missed the train.
lines.note: such + adj + sustantivo incontable + that + resultado

lines.text: It was so a cold day that we stayed inside.
lines.note: Incorrecto: so no va antes de a + sustantivo → usa such

tip.label: Equivalencia:
tip.body: so…that = «tan… que» con adjetivos/adverbios. such…that = «tan… que» cuando hay sustantivo.
